items.headline: LeBron James isn't worried yet after Lakers lose 2nd straight potential series clincher to Rockets
items.description:
LOS ANGELES (AP) — LeBron James didn't get overly excited last week when his Los Angeles Lakers rolled to a shocking 3-0 series lead over the Houston Rockets despite playing without their top two scorers.
James also isn't ready to panic after his Lakers lost their second straight potential clincher Wednesday night, putting them halfway to making an unwanted bit of NBA history.

items.headline: LAFC beats Toluca 2-1 in CONCACAF Champions Cup semi-final first leg
items.description: Nkosi Tafari's late header gave Los Angeles FC a 2-1 win in the first leg of their CONCACAF Champions Cup semi-final...

items.headline: Wrexham aims to keep alive Premier League dream by securing playoff spot in Championship finale
items.description:
For Wrexham’s growing global fanbase, it’s set to be another heart-stopping end to the season.
This time, a place in the Premier League is at stake.

items.headline: Howden's short-handed goal gives Golden Knights 5-4 double-OT win over Mammoth and 3-2 series lead
items.description:
LAS VEGAS (AP) — Brett Howden scored a short-handed goal at 5:28 of the second overtime Wednesday night to give Vegas a 5-4 victory over the Utah Mammoth and put the Golden Knights one victory away from winning the first-round series.
The Golden Knights take a 3-2 lead into their best-of-seven NHL playoffs series on Friday night in Salt Lake City.

items.headline: Schroder's halftime speech, 11 points in 4th quarter spark Cavaliers to victory over Raptors
items.description:
CLEVELAND (AP) — James Harden garnered the most attention in the series of moves the Cleveland Cavaliers made near the NBA trade deadline.
However, the Cavaliers would have been in dire straits Wednesday night without Dennis Schroder.

items.headline: Rockets beat Lakers 99-93 in Game 5, avoiding playoff elimination for the 2nd straight game
items.description:
LOS ANGELES (AP) — Jabari Smith Jr. scored 22 points, Tari Eason added 18 and the Houston Rockets avoided playoff elimination for the second straight game with a 99-93 victory over the Lakers in Game 5 on Wednesday night, trimming Los Angeles' lead in the first-round series to 3-2.
Alperen Sengun had 14 points, nine rebounds and eight assists for the fifth-seeded Rockets, who sent the series back to Houston for Game 6 on Friday night with a gritty performance to overcome Austin Reaves' return to the Lakers.

items.headline: Butler homers, Kurtz extends walk streak to 18 games; Athletics beat Royals 5-2
items.description:
WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Lawrence Butler hit a three-run home run, Luis Severino gave up one run and struck out eight in seven innings, and the Athletics beat Kansas City 5-2 on Wednesday night to snap the Royals' four-game win streak.
Nick Kurtz hit an RBI double and walked, his 18th consecutive game with a walk, extending his franchise record and tying him with Barry Bonds for the longest such single-season streak in nearly 80 years. Bonds walked in each of his final 18 games in 2002.

items.headline: Nathan Church steals another homer, this time giving the Cardinals a win over the Pirates
items.description:
PITTSBURGH (AP) — Nathan Church is making a habit of stealing home runs.
The rookie left fielder's latest saved the game for the St. Louis Cardinals.

items.headline: Texier breaks 3rd-period tie, Canadiens beat Lightning in Game 5 to take series lead
items.description:
TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Alexandre Texier broke a tie 1:06 into the third period and the Montreal Canadiens beat the Tampa Bay Lightning 3-2 on Wednesday night to take a 3-2 lead in the first-round series.
Rookie Jakub Dobes stopped 38 shots to help Montreal move within a victory of advancing for the first time since losing to the Lightning in the Stanley Cup Finals in 2021.

items.headline: Olson's 2-run walk-off homer sends Braves over Tigers 4-3
items.description:
ATLANTA (AP) — Matt Olson hit a two-run homer in the ninth inning off Kenley Jansen, ruining a dominant performance by Tarik Skubal and rallying the Atlanta Braves to a 4-3 victory over the Detroit Tigers on Wednesday night.
Jansen, who blew his third save in nine chances, walked Ozzie Albies to lead off the ninth. Olson followed with his ninth homer, a shot into Atlanta’s bullpen to end the game.
